seoTitle: How to Do MLA Format in Google Docs (2026)
metaDescription: Learn how to set up MLA formatting in Google Docs, including margins, double spacing, the running head, and hanging indents for Works Cited.
intro: Formatting an essay or research paper to MLA standards involves several small but important details, from margins to hanging indents. This guide walks through setting up each element correctly inside Google Docs. It's for students and writers preparing an MLA-compliant academic document.

steps.heading: Set margins and font
steps.body: Adjust your margins to exactly one inch on all sides, then switch your font to Times New Roman at 12pt.

steps.heading: Apply double spacing
steps.body: Click Format, hover over Line & Paragraph Spacing, and select Double.

steps.heading: Add your identification block and title
steps.body: Type your four-line identification block on the left margin, then center your paper's title above the body text.

steps.heading: Set up the running head
steps.body: Double-click the header area, insert page numbers aligned to the top right, and type your last name right next to the page number.

steps.heading: Format your Works Cited page
steps.body: Start a new page, center the label Works Cited, list your sources, then drag the ruler's left indent handle to 0.5 inches for a hanging indent.

faq.question: What font and size does MLA require?
faq.answer: MLA format calls for a standard, readable font like Times New Roman at 12pt.

faq.question: Do I need page numbers on every page?
faq.answer: Yes, MLA requires a running head with your last name and page number on every page, including the first.

faq.question: How do I create a hanging indent for citations?
faq.answer: Highlight your reference list and drag the ruler's left indent marker to 0.5 inches while keeping the first line at the margin.

faq.question: Should my title be bold or underlined?
faq.answer: MLA style typically calls for a plain, centered title without bold, underline, or italics unless referencing another work's title.
